exemplar.title: How do the contemporary Indonesian coming-of-age films,"Nanti Kita Cerita Tentang Hari Ini"(2020) and "Keluarga Cemara (2018), represent family struggles in the context of Indonesian society through their distinct visual styles?"

detailedExemplarData.title: How do the contemporary Indonesian coming-of-age films,"Nanti Kita Cerita Tentang Hari Ini"(2020) and "Keluarga Cemara (2018), represent family struggles in the context of Indonesian society through their distinct visual styles?"

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with a clear and appropriate focus on visual style and family struggle.
The research question is clearly stated, wellâfocused, and effectively connected to the structure of the essay.
The methodology draws on an appropriate range of relevant primary film analysis and secondary sources, with evidence of informed selection.
weaknesses: Scope is somewhat overâbroad, combining two films and multiple visual parameters, which occasionally dilutes analytical depth.
Methodological rationale is not fully articulated and lacks sustained engagement with formal filmâstudies theory.
Sections of historical context sometimes detract from the core analytical focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates a strong, sustained focus on the declared topic and research question, with an appropriate methodological approach. Minor lapses in scope management and theoretical framing prevent it from reaching the maximum band, but overall performance merits 5 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates clear subject knowledge of Indonesian cinema history and filmic concepts.
Applies terminology such as mise-en-scÃ¨ne, framing, and motivated lighting accurately and consistently to support analysis.
Links technical visual elements to thematic interpretations in the two films.
weaknesses: Reliance on journalistic and webâbased sources limits academic depth.
Some sections drift into descriptive background rather than analytical application to the research question.
Advanced theoretical concepts (e.g., semiotics, spectatorship) are under-utilized.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows sound and mostly consistent application of subject knowledge and film terminology, with clear relevance to the research question. Occasional descriptive tangents and limited theoretical depth justify a midâband score of 4 out of 6.

strengths: Provides coherent comparative analysis linking specific visual choices (lighting, composition, palette) to familyâstruggle themes.
Uses frame stills and contextual sources effectively to support analytical claims.
Constructs a logical argument across both films, synthesizing findings in a focused conclusion.
weaknesses: Engagement with alternative interpretations and counterâarguments is brief and could be more critically explorative.
Key academic filmâstudies texts on socio-cultural theory are missing, limiting theoretical rigor.
Some analysis lapses into repetitive description and could deepen evaluation of broader implications.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work presents good to excellent critical thinking: research is largely relevant, analysis is well supported, and the discussion forms a coherent argument. Shortcomings in critical evaluation and theoretical engagement place the criterion solidly above midpoint but below top performance, warranting 8 out of 12.

strengths: The essay follows a clearly appropriate structure with a title page, table of contents, labeled sections, and works-cited list.
Visual materials are integrated with figure numbers and captions close to the text discussion.
Formatting largely conforms to IB expectations for fonts, margins, and layout.
weaknesses: In-text citation conventions are inconsistently applied (missing dates, incomplete references).
Page numbers are absent in some sections of the table of contents, reducing navigability.
Long quotations and screenshots occasionally disrupt the flow of the narrative.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Most IB formatting and organizational requirements are met, and the structure supports readability. However, citation inconsistencies and pagination issues prevent a topâband award, resulting in 3 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Use of MLA layout elements.** The essay presents a consistent font, line-spacing and margin, supplies a complete works-cited list, captions images, and reports an accurate word count.  
**Minor issues.** In-text citations are inconsistent (some web sources lack dates; a few images are captioned but not fully referenced), page numbers are not visible in the provided extract, and long quotations/screenshots sometimes disrupt flow.  
**Overall compliance.** Most but not all IB formatting prescriptions appear respected; consequently the work earns the lower mark of the top band (3/4).

**Clarity and suitability of topic.** The introduction immediately states that the essay will explore âhowâ two specific Indonesian coming-of-age films represent family struggles through visual style. The focus on mise-en-scÃ¨ne and cinematography is entirely appropriate for Film studies and the cultural framing is explicit.  
**Purpose and focus.** Throughout the table of contents and section headings (e.g., âMise-en-sceneâ, âCinematographyâ) the student keeps returning to the declared purpose of linking visual style to family struggle, showing a consistently signposted line of inquiry.  
**Minor limitations.** Occasional digressions into historical background and industry context are lengthier than needed and the topic could have been narrowed further (e.g., limiting the number of visual parameters or tightening the cultural lens). These small lapses keep the work just short of the very top of the band.

**Scope and relevance of research.** The student draws on primary film texts extensively and supplements them with contextual sources on Indonesian cinema history, production design interviews and critical articles. These sources are generally pertinent to the RQ and support points made.  
**Breadth vs. depth.** While the range is adequate, it is not extensive; key academic works on Asian cinema theory or family representation are missing, and citations are unevenly integrated. Still, the majority of evidence is relevant and sufficient to sustain the line of inquiry.  
**Overall quality.** The research is clearly more than âadequateâ but stops short of âconsistently excellentâ, placing it comfortably in the 4-6 band; a mid-high mark of 5 is appropriate.

**Range and relevance of sources/methods.** The student combines primary film analysis (detailed shot descriptions, stills) with a modest set of secondary sources (7 items, mostly online articles and one academic text). These are generally relevant to Indonesian cinema and visual analysis.  
**Evidence of informed selection.** Citations show some effort to ground claims in production-design interviews and scholarly commentary, indicating developing methodological awareness.  
**Gaps and unevenness.** The range of sources is limited (few scholarly works; heavy reliance on web articles and DVD extras). The essay lacks a clear explanation of how sources were selected, and there is no sustained engagement with established film-theory methodologies (e.g., semiotics, socio-historical theory). Thus, performance is competent but not consistently strong, fitting the mid-band.

**Discussion and argument.** The student constructs a generally coherent comparative argument, culminating in a conclusion that synthesises how each filmâs visual style communicates family struggles.  
**Evaluation and critical stance.** There is some evaluative commentâfor instance, on the effectiveness of sterile dÃ©cor or warm palettesâbut critical reflection on limitations, counter-arguments or alternative interpretations is brief. The essay tends to affirm rather than question its own readings.  
**Balance.** While the two films receive roughly equal attention, some sections (e.g., historical background) feel bolted on and are not critically weighed against the central thesis. Consequently, the work aligns with the upper end of the 4-6 band, meriting 6 rather than advancing into the 7-9 range.

**Organization and required elements.** The essay includes title page with word count, a clear table of contents, logical sectioning (Introduction, Background, Film Analyses, Comparative Analysis, Conclusion, Works Cited). Transitions between sections are generally smooth, and visual material is integrated with figure numbers and captions.  
**Cohesion.** Paragraphing is coherent; headings guide the reader effectively; images are placed near discussion points. Any minor redundancies (e.g., slightly overlapping sub-sections) do not detract significantly from readability.  
**Meets all requirements.** The structure fulfils IB EE expectations (abstract no longer required under current guide). Thus the work fits securely in the top 3-4 band; a score of 4 is justified.

**Demonstrated knowledge.** The essay displays sound understanding of Indonesian cinemaâs historical phases, key industry figures (e.g., Usmar Ismail, Joko Anwar) and core filmic concepts such as mise-en-scÃ¨ne, framing, motivated lighting and contrast.  
**Application to research question.** Most of this knowledge is applied to the two focus filmsâe.g., linking Adrianto Sinagaâs minimalist production design to emotional sterility or Yadi Sugandiâs desaturated palette to financial hardship. These links generally support the investigation.  
**Limitations.** Some sections drift into descriptive plot summary or national cinema background without tying explicitly to the analytical thrust. Secondary sources are invoked sparingly; several citations are journalistic rather than academic, which reduces the depth of theoretical engagement. Overall, the strand meets the descriptors for 3-4, with enough clarity to merit the upper mark.

**Depth of analysis.** The essay moves beyond description by linking specific visual choices (lighting temperature shifts, spatial composition, high-contrast flashbacks) to psychological states and socio-cultural pressures, showing a coherent analytical method.  
**Support and integration.** Most points are illustrated with frame stills and occasional secondary commentary, giving reasonable support to claims. There is clear comparative analysis that highlights similarities and differences between the two filmsâ treatment of family struggle.  
**Shortcomings.** Some analyses lapse into repetitive description and do not always develop broader implications for Indonesian society; evaluation of alternative readings is limited. These issues place the work at the lower end of the 7-9 band, hence a score of 7.

**Research question stated and generally focused.** The RQ is clearly presented on the title page and foregrounds the two films, the concept of family struggle and the analytical lens of âdistinct visual styles.â  
**Partial over-breadth.** By combining two feature-length films, two complex thematic areas (family struggle + Indonesian society) and two broad stylistic domains (all mise-en-scÃ¨ne and cinematography), the question risks becoming diffuse. This breadth occasionally leads to descriptive rather than analytical passages.  
**Connection to discussion.** Despite moments of sprawl, most sections do circle back to the RQ, and the conclusion explicitly addresses it. The focus could, however, be sharper and more consistently sustained, warranting the upper level of the 3-4 band rather than the highest band.

**Use of film terminology.** Terms such as âmise-en-scÃ¨ne,â âhand-held camera,â âmotivated lighting,â and âframe within a frameâ are employed regularly and usually accurately. This enables the reader to follow the technical discussion.  
**Conceptual handling.** The student differentiates clearly between visual elements (set design, lighting, framing) and their thematic connotations, demonstrating a functional command of key film-studies concepts.  
**Consistency and precision.** There are occasional lapsesâsome concepts (e.g., âhigh-contrast cinematographyâ) are used rather loosely, and theoretical vocabulary (semiotics, spectatorship) is absent. Nevertheless, accuracy outweighs error, justifying a solid mark in the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The research question is stated prominently and communicates the investigationâs focus on visual style and family struggle in two films.
Combining two films and multiple visual dimensions risks an overly broad scope; consider narrowing the question to one film or a single visual element for sharper focus.
The title of the investigation is clearly stated at the start, meeting the IB requirement for a visible and descriptive title page.
The accurate word count appears on the title page and confirms compliance with the maximum 4000-word limit.
The table of contents is clear and well-organized, facilitating navigation through the essayâs structure.
The introduction outline in the contents aligns directly with your research question, showing good alignment between structure and investigation focus.
The extensive sub-headings under background suggest potential over-emphasis on context which may detract from the core film analysis. Consider pruning secondary details.
An in-text citation ends abruptly at âVisinema Picturesâ; complete this reference and ensure all sources are fully cited.
The table of contents spans multiple pages without page numbers, which reduces usability. Including pagination would improve clarity.
The "Works Cited" heading is clearly labeled, ensuring sources are easily located for reference verification.
The opening paragraph offers broad context but lacks a concise thesis statement; explicitly link background information to your research question here.
The second paragraph effectively previews how specific visual styles will signal family dynamics, demonstrating subject knowledge and conceptual clarity.
The narrative of character struggles is detailed but overly descriptive; elevate this by explicitly analyzing how set, lighting and framing convey these emotions.
The introduction section is clearly labeled and conforms to expected academic structure, aiding reader orientation.
The sentence ends abruptly, indicating a proofreading oversight. Ensure all paragraphs are complete and transitions are seamless.
The background on contemporary Indonesian cinema demonstrates strong subject knowledge and situates the films in a historical context.
The historical overview is detailed but drifts from the research focus. Tighten this section to foreground analysis of the two chosen films more quickly.
Terminology such as semiotics and spectatorship is absent. Integrate more established film-theory vocabulary to deepen theoretical engagement.
The shift to discussing visual aspects is well signposted, showing clear evidence of critical thinking in linking cinematic form to thematic purpose.
The section heading for the first film analysis is appropriately formatted and signals a new analytical focus.
Linking industry contributions to film analysis shows effective use of sources in context, indicating developing methodological awareness.
The film context description is informative but leans toward summary; sharpen analytical commentary by tying details directly to visual style and theme.
The second filmâs context is clearly segmented, maintaining parallel structure for comparative analysis.
The comparative analysis section is introduced clearly, setting up a structured evaluation of visual style across both films.
This paragraph efficiently outlines the comparative method, linking mise en scÃ¨ne elements to thematic representation in both films.
This thematic summary demonstrates sound knowledge of film context and supports later analytical comparisons.
Sub-section labeling for set design is precise and aids navigation through layered analytical content.
A standalone sentence appears disconnected from surrounding text; improve flow by integrating orphaned sentences into cohesive paragraphs.
The conceptual definition of mise en scÃ¨ne is clear but could be enriched by integrating film-theory terms such as semiotic analysis or spatial syntax.
Figures are captioned descriptively, but the analysis lacks direct linkage to the research question; explicitly discuss how each image supports your argument.
This detailed image analysis effectively connects visual elements of setting to themes of resilience and emotional change.
